agent.root_goal: Build a full-stack todo app: design schema, implement backend API, implement frontend, write integration tests.
agent.task: Build a full-stack todo app: design schema, implement backend API, implement frontend, write integration tests.

action.reasoning: The task of building a full-stack todo app is complex and naturally decomposes into four distinct phases: schema design, backend implementation, frontend implementation, and integration testing. These phases have clear dependencies: the backend depends on the schema, the frontend depends on the backend API, and the tests depend on both. I will spawn subtasks for each phase to allow for parallel work where possible (e.g., schema design can start immediately, backend can start once schema is done, etc.) and ensure each subtask has a clear goal and output. I will also take a self-role to integrate the final outputs and ensure the project is cohesive.

subtasks.goal: Design the database schema for the todo app.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask defines the data model for the todo app, including tables for users and todos. It serves the ROOT GOAL by providing the foundational structure for the backend and frontend.

outputs.description: SQL schema definition for the todo app.

subtasks.goal: Implement the backend API for the todo app.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask builds the server-side logic, including endpoints for CRUD operations on todos. It depends on the schema design and serves the ROOT GOAL by providing the API for the frontend.

outputs.description: Compressed backend code including server, routes, and database connection.

subtasks.goal: Implement the frontend for the todo app.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask builds the client-side UI, including components for displaying and managing todos. It depends on the backend API and serves the ROOT GOAL by providing the user interface.

outputs.description: Compressed frontend code including components, styles, and API integration.

subtasks.goal: Write integration tests for the todo app.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask creates tests to verify the interaction between the frontend and backend. It depends on both the backend and frontend code and serves the ROOT GOAL by ensuring the app works correctly.

outputs.description: Compressed integration test code and test results.

self_role.goal: Integrate the outputs from all subtasks and provide a final summary.

outputs.description: A summary of the full-stack todo app, including links to all artifacts.

self_role.condition: done("root.1/schema.sql") AND done("root.2/backend_code.tar.gz") AND done("root.3/frontend_code.tar.gz") AND done("root.4/integration_tests.tar.gz")

agent.capsule: This subtask builds the server-side logic, including endpoints for CRUD operations on todos. It depends on the schema design and serves the ROOT GOAL by providing the API for the frontend.

action.reasoning: The task is to implement a backend API based on a provided SQL schema. This is a standard, well-defined programming task that can be completed in a single focused effort. The schema is available in shared memory, so there are no missing dependencies. Spawning subtasks for individual endpoints would incur unnecessary overhead for a task of this scope. Therefore, I will execute the task directly, generating the backend code and packaging it as required.

result_outputs.description: Compressed backend code including server, routes, and database connection.

agent.root_goal: Build a full-stack todo app: design schema, implement backend API, implement frontend, write integration tests.
agent.task: Implement the frontend for the todo app.
agent.capsule: This subtask builds the client-side UI, including components for displaying and managing todos. It depends on the backend API and serves the ROOT GOAL by providing the user interface.

expected_outputs.description: Compressed frontend code including components, styles, and API integration.

action.reasoning: The task is to implement the frontend for a todo app. This is a substantial task involving multiple components (list, form, individual items), state management, and API integration. It naturally decomposes into parallel subtasks for different UI concerns, followed by an integration step to bundle everything. I will spawn subtasks for the core components and the API service layer, then take the self-role of integrating them into the final deliverable.

subtasks.goal: Implement the API service layer and state management for the frontend.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask creates the abstraction layer for communicating with the backend API (root.2) and manages the application state (loading, error, todo list). It serves the ROOT GOAL by ensuring the frontend can reliably fetch and mutate todo data.

outputs.description: JavaScript module containing API client functions for CRUD operations on todos.
JavaScript module handling application state and side effects.

subtasks.goal: Implement the core UI components for the todo app.
subtasks.capsule: This subtask builds the visual components: TodoList, TodoItem, and AddTodoForm. It serves the ROOT GOAL by providing the interactive user interface elements that users will see and use.

outputs.description: JavaScript/JSX file containing the React components for the todo UI.
CSS file containing styles for the todo app components.

self_role.goal: Integrate the API service, state management, and UI components into a complete frontend application and package it.

outputs.description: Compressed frontend code including components, styles, and API integration.

self_role.condition: done("root.3.1/api_service.js") AND done("root.3.1/state_manager.js") AND done("root.3.2/components.js") AND done("root.3.2/styles.css")

action.reasoning: The task requires implementing two distinct but related JavaScript modules: an API service layer and a state manager. These are small enough to be implemented in a single focused effort without the overhead of spawning sub-agents. The API service handles HTTP requests, while the state manager handles local state updates and side effects. I will implement both files directly.

JavaScript module handling application state and side effects.

action.reasoning: The task is to write integration tests for the todo app. This depends on the backend code (root.2) and frontend code (root.3) being available. Since these artifacts are listed in relevant memory, I can proceed. Writing integration tests is a focused task that involves setting up a test environment, mocking or spinning up the backend, interacting with the frontend, and asserting correctness. It does not naturally decompose into independent parallel subtasks that would benefit from spawning, as the test suite is typically a cohesive unit. Therefore, I will execute this task myself.

result_outputs.description: Compressed integration test code and test results.
